{"id":"da-mv2","title":"Scheduled report generation via Celery beat","description":"Add scheduled report generation using Celery beat. Backend: new ReportSchedule model (template_id FK, cron expression or interval, date_range_mode enum like 'last_7d/last_30d/last_quarter/custom', enabled flag, last_run_at, next_run_at, delivery method: s3_store/email/both, recipients list). Alembic migration. Celery beat task that checks for due schedules, generates PDF using the linked template config, stores in S3 (MinIO), optionally emails. API endpoints: CRUD for schedules (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/reports/schedules), GET /api/reports/generated to list past generated reports, GET /api/reports/generated/{id}/download. Frontend: schedule management UI on Reports page - create schedule from template, set interval, toggle enabled, view generation history. Depends on report templates feature.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"crashish@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-20T09:58:02Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-20T09:58:02Z"}

{"id":"da-1xv","title":"Fix emoji rendering in PDF reports","description":"PDF reports render emojis as generic box characters. weasyprint needs a font with emoji coverage. Options: 1) Add Noto Color Emoji font to the Docker image and reference it in the report CSS @font-face, or 2) Strip/replace emojis before rendering. Preferred: install fonts-noto-color-emoji in Dockerfile and add font-family fallback in report.html CSS. Test with findings that contain emoji-heavy content (Bank Loading USA, etc).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"crashish@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-20T10:13:53Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-20T10:13:53Z"}

{"id":"da-z3x","title":"Highlight matched terms in report findings detail","description":"The findings detail section in PDF reports currently dumps raw matched_terms JSON including start/end highlight offsets. Instead: 1) Use the start/end values to wrap matched substrings in the raw_content with \u003cmark\u003e tags (or styled spans) so they appear highlighted in the report. 2) Show matched term values as readable badges/pills rather than JSON. 3) In the template, replace the current matched_terms JSON dump with highlighted raw_content text and a clean 'Matched: term1, term2' line. The highlight offsets reference positions in the raw_content field. Handle overlapping ranges and HTML-escape content before inserting highlight markup.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"crashish@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-20T10:14:21Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-20T10:14:21Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"attached_molecule: da-wisp-16f4
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-20T10:15:44Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

The findings detail section in PDF reports currently dumps raw matched_terms JSON including start/end highlight offsets. Instead: 1) Use the start/end values to wrap matched substrings in the raw_content with <mark> tags (or styled spans) so they appear highlighted in the report. 2) Show matched term values as readable badges/pills rather than JSON. 3) In the template, replace the current matched_terms JSON dump with highlighted raw_content text and a clean 'Matched: term1, term2' line. The highlight offsets reference positions in the raw_content field. Handle overlapping ranges and HTML-escape content before inserting highlight markup."}

Instead: 1) Use the start/end values to wrap matched substrings in the raw_content with \u003cmark\u003e tags (or styled spans) so they appear highlighted in the report. 2) Show matched term values as readable badges/pills rather than JSON. 3) In the template, replace the current matched_terms JSON dump with highlighted raw_content text and a clean 'Matched: term1, term2' line. The highlight offsets reference positions in the raw_content field. Handle overlapping ranges and HTML-escape content before inserting highlight markup.","status":"hooked","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"darkdisco/polecats/nitro","owner":"crashish@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-20T10:14:21Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-20T10:15:44Z"}

{"id":"da-3qn","title":"BIN database integration for card issuer identification","description":"Integrate a comprehensive BIN (Bank Identification Number) database into darkdisco for identifying card issuers from BIN prefixes found in CC dump findings.

Architecture:
1. New bin_database table: bin_prefix (varchar, indexed), bin_range_start, bin_range_end (for range-based lookups), issuer_name, card_brand (Visa/Mastercard/Amex/Discover/etc), card_type (credit/debit/prepaid), card_level (classic/gold/platinum/business), country_code, country_name, bank_url, bank_phone, source (which dataset it came from), updated_at
2. Data ingestion pipeline:
   - Parse Visa BIN range PDFs (Visa publishes BIN Table updates)
   - Parse Mastercard BIN range PDFs (Mastercard publishes similar)
   - Import from open BIN databases (binlist.net API as fallback, or static CSV datasets)
   - Management command: bd bin-import --source visa --file visa_bins.pdf
   - Management command: bd bin-import --source csv --file bins.csv
3. BIN lookup API: GET /api/bins/lookup/{prefix} returns issuer info
4. Auto-enrichment: when a finding contains BIN-matchable numbers (6-8 digit prefixes), auto-lookup and annotate the finding metadata with issuer info for all detected BINs
5. Frontend: BIN lookup tool page, and BIN issuer display in finding detail when card numbers are detected
6. PDF parsing: use pdfplumber or camelot to extract BIN range tables from Visa/MC PDFs. Handle both static prefix lists and range formats (e.g., 400000-400099 = Bank X).

Modern BIN standard is 8 digits (IIN), but legacy 6-digit prefixes still in wide use. Support both.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"crashish@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-21T18:44:56Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-21T18:44:56Z"}

{"description":"attached_molecule: da-wisp-azz2h
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work

formula_vars: base_branch=main

Integrate a comprehensive BIN (Bank Identification Number) database into darkdisco for identifying card issuers from BIN prefixes found in CC dump findings.

Architecture:
1. New bin_database table: bin_prefix (varchar, indexed), bin_range_start, bin_range_end (for range-based lookups), issuer_name, card_brand (Visa/Mastercard/Amex/Discover/etc), card_type (credit/debit/prepaid), card_level (classic/gold/platinum/business), country_code, country_name, bank_url, bank_phone, source (which dataset it came from), updated_at
2. Data ingestion pipeline:
   - Parse Visa BIN range PDFs (Visa publishes BIN Table updates)
   - Parse Mastercard BIN range PDFs (Mastercard publishes similar)
   - Import from open BIN databases (binlist.net API as fallback, or static CSV datasets)
   - Management command: bd bin-import --source visa --file visa_bins.pdf
   - Management command: bd bin-import --source csv --file bins.csv
3. BIN lookup API: GET /api/bins/lookup/{prefix} returns issuer info
4. Auto-enrichment: when a finding contains BIN-matchable numbers (6-8 digit prefixes), auto-lookup and annotate the finding metadata with issuer info for all detected BINs
5. Frontend: BIN lookup tool page, and BIN issuer display in finding detail when card numbers are detected
6. PDF parsing: use pdfplumber or camelot to extract BIN range tables from Visa/MC PDFs. Handle both static prefix lists and range formats (e.g., 400000-400099 = Bank X).

Modern BIN standard is 8 digits (IIN), but legacy 6-digit prefixes still in wide use. Support both."}

{"id":"da-zch","title":"File browser pagination: add page controls and fix hardcoded page_size=200","description":"Frontend Files.tsx hardcodes page=1\u0026page_size=200, showing only the 200 most recent files. There are 999+ extracted files but only 200 visible.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-23T17:45:49Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-23T17:45:49Z"}

{"id":"da-6qe","title":"Matching filters management UI","description":"Add a Settings/Filters page to the frontend for managing matching_filters.yaml content via the API. Features: (1) View/add/edit/delete negative patterns with regex syntax highlighting. (2) View/add/edit/delete fraud indicators. (3) Test panel: paste sample text and see which patterns match/suppress. (4) Show hit counts per pattern (requires backend tracking). Needs new API endpoints: GET/PUT /settings/matching-filters for CRUD, POST /settings/matching-filters/test for pattern testing. Backend should read/write data/matching_filters.yaml and reload the matcher. React/TS frontend at frontend/src/, existing API at src/darkdisco/api/routes.py.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-26T10:08:10Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-26T10:08:10Z"}

Also check max_archive_size (100MB) in stealer_log.py:14 and align it. {"id":"da-4cm","title":"Recursive extraction in stream_extract_from_s3 for nested archives","description":"extract_archive() (in-memory path) recurses into nested archives up to _MAX_ARCHIVE_DEPTH. But stream_extract_from_s3() (disk-based path for large files, line 341 in files.py) does NOT recurse — it just os.walks flat output. Nested zips inside large stealer log archives are listed as files but never opened. Need to add recursive extraction to the streaming path. Related: da-3om was filed for this but never started.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:39:08Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-27T20:39:08Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"attached_molecule: da-wisp-dtk8h
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-27T20:40:43Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

extract_archive() (in-memory path) recurses into nested archives up to _MAX_ARCHIVE_DEPTH. But stream_extract_from_s3() (disk-based path for large files, line 341 in files.py) does NOT recurse — it just os.walks flat output. Nested zips inside large stealer log archives are listed as files but never opened. Need to add recursive extraction to the streaming path. Related: da-3om was filed for this but never started."}
